meta.notes: Starter content packs for Game Shelf + Sparks. {name} = insert a random circle member. Tone: Gen Z, Indian-context-friendly, PG-13, no m-dashes. Seed into Firestore gameContent/{deckId}.

sparks_daily.prompt: would you rather your search history got leaked to the group or your camera roll?
would you rather always be 30 mins late or always be 2 hours early?
would you rather only eat maggi for a year or never eat maggi again?
would you rather fight one horse-sized pigeon or fifty pigeon-sized horses?
would you rather have unlimited paneer or unlimited wifi?
would you rather your parents read this chat or your crush?
would you rather live one floor above your bestie or one city away from everyone?
would you rather never use emojis again or only communicate in emojis?
would you rather get caught singing in an auto or dancing in an elevator?
would you rather have your life narrated by a cricket commentator or a saas-bahu serial voiceover?
what is the most unhinged thing in your camera roll right now? describe it, cowards
which member of this circle would survive a horror movie and who dies first?
drop the last song you played. no skipping to look cool
what would this group's reality show be called?
confess a food opinion that could end friendships
what were you doing exactly one year ago? check your gallery
if this group had a group tattoo what would it be?
rate your week out of 10 and give one headline
what is a smell that instantly teleports you to childhood?
which app would you delete from everyone else's phone?
send the 7th photo in your gallery. no context allowed
voice note yourself saying good morning in the most dramatic way possible
recreate your oldest profile picture and post both
text your mom something nice and screenshot her reply
everyone drop their screen time. shame will be distributed accordingly
describe your day using only 3 emojis. others must decode
drop a photo of your current view. yes right now
compliment the person above you but make it weirdly specific
share the last thing you googled. we accept your resignation
predict what {name} is doing right now. {name} must confirm or deny

roles.snake: you are a Snake. sabotage missions quietly. deny everything. trust no one, especially the other snake
roles.loyal: you are Loyal. figure out who the snakes are before they ruin everything. good luck, detective

narrator_lines: the circle gathers. two among you are snakes. act natural
mission time. choose your squad wisely, captain
the votes are in. someone is sweating
mission failed. a snake was on that squad. the accusations may begin
mission passed. either no snakes went, or they are playing the long game
three failures. the snakes win. betrayal tastes delicious
three successes. the loyals win. the snakes slither back into the group chat

hot_takes: biryani without raita is superior
voice notes over 1 minute should be illegal
window seat is overrated
cold coffee is better than hot coffee
breakfast food is the worst meal category
watching a movie at home beats the theatre
gulab jamun clears rasmalai
small weddings are better than big fat ones
monsoon is the best season, fight me
pineapple belongs on pizza
double texting is completely fine
reading the book is not always better than the movie
birthdays after 21 are just admin work
cats are better roommates than dogs
the aisle seat is the power seat
maggi is better slightly undercooked
beach vacation beats mountain vacation
9 AM classes should be a human rights violation
paying extra for guac or cheese is always worth it
group video calls are worse than texting
new year resolutions are pointless
sending memes is a love language
it's okay to walk out of a bad movie
chai clears coffee and it's not close
physical books beat kindle
surprise parties are actually stressful
winter needs to be earned, summer people are stronger
leaving someone on read is sometimes self care
street food beats fine dining
the middle of the samosa is the best part, not the corners
planning the trip is more fun than the trip
you should never go to a concert just for stories
answering calls from unknown numbers is a personality trait
restaurants with huge menus can't be trusted
the best conversations happen after midnight

openers: the wifi went out at exactly 3:33 AM and that's when we heard it
nobody expected the wedding to end with a police chase
it started as a normal Tuesday until the pigeon spoke
the group trip was going great until someone checked the bill
deep in the office fridge, something had been growing for weeks
she opened the tiffin and immediately regretted everything
the last auto in the city had one rule: no questions
on the 47th floor, the elevator stopped between worlds
the street dog had been following them for three cities now
it was the biggest jackpot in history and the ticket was in the laundry
the family group chat had been silent for six days. too silent
at the stroke of midnight, every phone in the hostel rang at once
the recipe called for one ingredient nobody could pronounce
he woke up famous and had absolutely no idea why
the treasure map was drawn on the back of a canteen bill
every mirror in the mall showed a different version of her
the interview was going perfectly until the parrot started talking
somewhere between floor 2 and floor 3, the staircase changed
the monsoon brought rain, traffic, and one very lost penguin
grandma's old trunk was never supposed to be opened
the new roommate seemed normal except for the humming at 4 AM
it was just a food review until the chef sat down at their table
the last message on the phone said: don't come home yet
the school reunion had one uninvited guest nobody recognized
the vending machine started dispensing things that didn't exist

dare_mild: talk in a news anchor voice for the next 3 rounds
do your best impression of another member until someone guesses who
let the group set your phone wallpaper for 24 hours
eat a spoon of any condiment the group picks
do 15 pushups or attempt them dramatically
speak only in questions for the next 5 minutes
show everyone your most used emojis
let someone draw on your hand with a pen
sing everything you say for the next 2 rounds
do your best runway walk across the room
balance a book on your head until your next turn
let the group scroll your gallery for 20 seconds (you can prepare, coward)
do a dramatic slow-motion replay of how you entered the room
compliment everyone in the room in under 30 seconds
hold an intense eye contact staring contest with the person opposite
dare_spicy: call a friend not in this room and sing them happy birthday. it is not their birthday
text your most recent chat 'we need to talk' and don't reply for 10 minutes
post a story chosen by the group (nothing mean, nothing permanent)
let the group send one message from your phone to a person they choose (group approves it's harmless)
do your most accurate impression of your own parent
reenact your most embarrassing moment with full commitment
let the person to your right style your hair right now
speak in a different accent until someone forgets and laughs
show the group your screen time and defend it like a lawyer
call a restaurant and ask a genuinely weird but polite question
swap one clothing item with another player for the rest of the game
let the group pick your profile picture for the next 24 hours
dramatically read your last sent voice note transcript out loud
dance with zero music for 30 straight seconds
let the group write your status/bio for one day
dare_chaos: let the group post one story from your account (they must be kind, you must not look)
eat a snack blindfolded and guess what it is while narrating like a food critic
perform a one minute standup set about this friend group
recreate a famous movie scene, group picks the movie, you have 60 seconds to prep
let two members have a full conversation using only your face as the reactor
improvise a dramatic breakup scene with a household object
give a TED talk on a topic the group picks, 90 seconds, full confidence
let the group set one alarm on your phone for any time with any label
become the group's butler for the next 3 rounds, in character
do a dramatic reading of the group chat's oldest messages you can find
make up and perform a 15 second jingle about the person opposite you
narrate everything anyone does like a nature documentary for 5 minutes
let the group rename you in their contacts, permanent until next hangout
walk to the kitchen and back as 3 different characters
freestyle rap using 3 words the group gives you. no mercy

kings_cup_rules.rule: waterfall: everyone drinks in a chain, nobody stops before the person on their right
you: pick someone to take a sip
me: that's a sip for you, champion
floor: last one to touch the floor sips
heaven: last one to point up sips
mate: pick a mate, they sip whenever you do, till the end
rhyme: say a word, go around rhyming, whoever blanks sips
categories: pick a category, whoever repeats or blanks sips
rule master: invent a rule, breakers sip until the next Jack
question master: anyone who answers your questions sips, until the next Queen
pour a bit into the King's Cup. whoever draws the 4th King drinks the whole cursed mixture

kings_cup_zero_proof_forfeits: speak in an accent till your next turn
give a genuine compliment to everyone
do your worst dance move with full sincerity
let the group take one photo of you
talk without using the word 'like' till your next turn (fail = extra forfeit)
sing the next sentence you speak
let someone reposition you like a mannequin
narrate your next action like a movie trailer
hold your arms up till your next turn

roles.desc: eliminate one citizen each night. lie beautifully by day
save one person each night. yes, you can save yourself, hero
investigate one person each night. the app tells you the truth
citizen, but you MUST object loudly to every accusation. every single one
survive. suspect everyone. trust your gut and your worst friend the least

narrator_script: night falls on the city. everyone close your eyes. no peeking, we can literally see you
mafia, wake up. choose your victim in silence. blink twice for drama
mafia, sleep. doctor, wake up. choose someone to save
doctor, sleep. detective, wake up. point at your suspect. the app shall whisper the truth
everyone wake up. the city stirs. and someone... is gone
discuss. accuse. betray. you have 3 minutes
the votes are locked. the town has spoken
the accused was innocent. the mafia laughs quietly
the accused was MAFIA. the town celebrates. briefly
the mafia has taken the city. crime pays, apparently
the citizens prevail. justice, served with drama

this group edition: things involving people in this room

wyr_irl: would you rather swap phones with the person on your left for an hour or show your gallery for 1 minute?
would you rather let the group order your next meal or pick your outfit tomorrow?
would you rather speak only in whispers or only in announcements for the rest of the hangout?
would you rather give up your chair forever tonight or fetch everyone's water all night?
would you rather do 20 squats now or sing one full song later?
would you rather let someone here text from your phone or post one story?
would you rather eat the spiciest thing here or the weirdest food combo the group makes?
would you rather narrate everything you do for 10 minutes or be narrated by the group?
would you rather trade one clothing item or one accessory with someone here?
would you rather be blindfolded for the next round or have your hands tied for it?
would you rather let the group rename your contacts or rearrange your home screen?
would you rather demonstrate your shower singing or your alone-dancing?
would you rather share your screen time or your battery percentage anxiety live?
would you rather laugh at everything for 5 minutes or be forbidden from laughing?
would you rather hold hands with the person opposite for a round or maintain eye contact for 60 seconds?
would you rather give a toast about the host or a roast about yourself?
would you rather everyone sees your most played song or your most watched video?
would you rather sit on the floor for the rest of the night or stand for 30 minutes?
would you rather let the group choose your haircut description or your bio for a week?
would you rather taste test with your nose blocked or guess snacks blindfolded?
would you rather talk in third person or refer to everyone by full names all night?
would you rather reenact how you all met or predict where you'll all be in 10 years, dramatically?
would you rather do the next dare twice or two truths at chaos level?
would you rather be the group's waiter or the group's DJ for the night?
would you rather show your last 5 emojis or your last 3 searches?
would you rather arm wrestle the strongest person here or thumb war the fastest?
would you rather everyone imitates you for 5 minutes or you imitate everyone once?
would you rather eat dessert first forever or never eat dessert after meals?
would you rather text your 5th contact 'thinking of you' or call your 10th and hum?
would you rather the group picks your ringtone (loud, one week) or your alarm sound?
would you rather compliment a stranger tonight or ask one for a photo together?
would you rather redo your entrance to this hangout in slow motion or in fast forward?
would you rather swap seats every 10 minutes or never move again tonight?
would you rather share the last voice note you sent or the last one you received?
would you rather everyone rates your outfit honestly or your haircut honestly?
